The passengers landed in the UK on Sunday and none have reported symptoms, but they will will be monitored in hospital for 72 hours
President’s remarks came as Benjamin Netanyahu said the war was ‘not over’
As well as one who tested positive, another passenger has mild symptoms of hantavirus, the US health and human services department confirmed

items.title: Iran vows to fight on as Trump calls latest peace offer "unacceptable"
items.standfirst: Oil prices spike and the Strait of Hormuz stays paralyzed as Trump rejects Iran's peace proposal and Tehran vows to "fight whenever it is necessary."
items.content: Oil prices spike and the Strait of Hormuz stays paralyzed as Trump rejects Iran's peace proposal and Tehran vows to "fight whenever it is necessary."

items.title: Trump calls Iran's peace proposal "totally unacceptable" as gas prices rise
items.standfirst: President Trump on Sunday rejected Iran's latest peace proposal, calling it "totally unacceptable." With no agreement in sight, Trump officials for the first time over the weekend floated the idea of pausing the national gas tax as prices continue to rise.
items.content: President Trump on Sunday rejected Iran's latest peace proposal, calling it "totally unacceptable." With no agreement in sight, Trump officials for the first time over the weekend floated the idea of pausing the national gas tax as prices continue to rise.

items.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump rejects Iran response to US peace proposal as Tehran warns of new attacks
items.standfirst: US president calls Iranian response ‘totally unacceptable’ while Tehran says it will retaliate against any new US strikes or foreign warships in strait of HormuzTrump calls Iran’s response to peace plan ‘totally unacceptable’ as ceasefire fraysTurkey’s foreign minister, Hakan Fidan, will visit Qatar later today for talks on the war, its impact on the region and efforts to ensure navigational safety in the strait of Hormuz is resumed, a Turkish diplomatic source told the Reuters news agency.Turkey, which neighbours Iran, has been in close contact with the US, Iran and mediator Pakistan since the start of the conflict. It condemnded the US and Israel for launching the war, widely seen to have been done illegally, but also criticised Iran’s counter strikes on Gulf states. Continue reading...

It condemnded the US and Israel for launching the war, widely seen to have been done illegally, but also criticised Iran’s counter strikes on Gulf states. Continue reading...

items.title: Hantavirus ship evacuation nears completion as 94 flown home
items.standfirst: A complex day-long operation to repatriate occupants of a cruise ship struck by a deadly hantavirus outbreak neared completion late Sunday after 94 people of various nationalities were flown home from Spain's Canary Islands. Three passengers from the MV Hondius -- a Dutch husband and wife and a German woman -- have died, while others have fallen sick with the rare disease, which usually spreads among rodents. No vaccines or specific treatments exist for hantavirus, which is endemic in Argentina, where the ship departed in April.
items.content: A complex day-long operation to repatriate occupants of a cruise ship struck by a deadly hantavirus outbreak neared completion late Sunday after 94 people of various nationalities were flown home from Spain's Canary Islands. Three passengers from the MV Hondius -- a Dutch husband and wife and a German woman -- have died, while others have fallen sick with the rare disease, which usually spreads among rodents. No vaccines or specific treatments exist for hantavirus, which is endemic in Argentina, where the ship departed in April.

items.title: ‘Unacceptable’: What’s Iran’s peace proposal that Trump has rejected?
items.standfirst: On Sunday, Tehran sent a response to an earlier US proposal to end the war through mediator Pakistan.
items.content: On Sunday, Tehran sent a response to an earlier US proposal to end the war through mediator Pakistan.

items.title: More confirmed hantavirus cases: The situation in France
items.standfirst: A French ​passenger who was on a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak has tested positive for ​the ‌virus and her condition ⁠is deteriorating, French Health Minister Stephanie Rist said on ‌Monday. The passenger was among five French ⁠people who were on the ship. The four other passengers tested negative. So far French authorities have traced 22 contact cases. FRANCE 24's Environment Editor Valerie Dekimpe tells us more.
items.content: A French ​passenger who was on a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak has tested positive for ​the ‌virus and her condition ⁠is deteriorating, French Health Minister Stephanie Rist said on ‌Monday. The passenger was among five French ⁠people who were on the ship. The four other passengers tested negative. So far French authorities have traced 22 contact cases. FRANCE 24's Environment Editor Valerie Dekimpe tells us more.

items.title: American passenger who was on hantavirus-stricken cruise ship tests positive for virus
items.standfirst: One of the 17 American passengers evacuated from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship has tested positive for the virus. They are now under quarantine in Nebraska. Ramy Inocencio reports.
items.content: One of the 17 American passengers evacuated from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship has tested positive for the virus. They are now under quarantine in Nebraska. Ramy Inocencio reports.

items.title: Eye Opener: Americans on hantavirus cruise ship back in U.S.
items.standfirst: Seventeen Americans from the cruise ship stricken with hantavirus have returned to the U.S. Plus, President Trump rejects Iran's latest peace proposal. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.
items.content: Seventeen Americans from the cruise ship stricken with hantavirus have returned to the U.S. Plus, President Trump rejects Iran's latest peace proposal. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

items.title: Trump calls Iran's latest peace proposal response "totally unacceptable"
items.standfirst: President Trump said Sunday on social media that he thinks Iran's response to the latest peace proposal is "totally unacceptable." Meanwhile, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told 60 Minutes that regime change is possible in Iran. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi and Courtney Kealy have more.
items.content: President Trump said Sunday on social media that he thinks Iran's response to the latest peace proposal is "totally unacceptable." Meanwhile, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told 60 Minutes that regime change is possible in Iran. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi and Courtney Kealy have more.

items.content: An American on the repatriation flight began showing symptoms of hantavirus and another "tested mildly PCR positive for the Andes virus," the Department of Health and Human Services says.

items.title: Americans exposed to hantavirus on cruise ship land in Nebraska, including 1 who tested positive
items.standfirst: The 17 Americans who were on the cruise ship hit with hantavirus landed in Omaha, Nebraska, early Monday morning, including one person who tested positive and another showing symptoms. CBS News' Ramy Inocencio has the latest, while Dr. Céline Gounder breaks down some key facts about hantavirus.

items.standfirst: The passengers landed in the UK on Sunday and none have reported symptoms, but they will will be monitored in hospital for 72 hours.
items.content: The passengers landed in the UK on Sunday and none have reported symptoms, but they will will be monitored in hospital for 72 hours.

items.title: Trump calls Iran’s response to peace plan ‘totally unacceptable’ as ceasefire frays | First Thing
items.standfirst: President’s remarks came as Benjamin Netanyahu said the war was ‘not over’. Plus, American passengers of the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship travel to NebraskaDon’t already get First Thing in your inbox? Sign up hereGood morning.Donald Trump has condemned an Iranian response to a US peace proposal as “totally unacceptable” as the month-old ceasefire appeared to be wearing thin.What is the US position on Iranian nuclear facilities? The US parameters for nuclear talks reportedly included a moratorium on Iranian nuclear enrichment for up to 20 years; the transfer overseas, possibly to the US, of Iran’s stockpile of highly enriched uranium (HEU), and the dismantling of Iranian nuclear facilities.What is the sticking point? According to the Wall Street Journal, the Iranian counter-proposal suggested a shorter moratorium, the export of part of the highly enriched uranium stockpile and the dilution of the rest, and refusal to accept the dismantling of facilities.How have markets reacted? After Trump rejected the counter-proposal on his Truth Social platform, there was a 4% jump in Brent crude on Monday to $105.50 a barrel, before it settled at $103.50.Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.What measures are being taken to control the spread? As well as one who tested positive, another passenger has mild symptoms of hantavirus, the US health and human services department confirmed. These passengers were travelling in the plane’s biocontainment units, it added.Is the World Health Organization mandating a quarantine? No – it has recommended, but not mandated, a 42-day quarantine once passengers have landed in their home countries. As some countries enforce stricter rules than others, here is how the responses vary. Continue reading...
items.content: President’s remarks came as Benjamin Netanyahu said the war was ‘not over’. Plus, American passengers of the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship travel to NebraskaDon’t already get First Thing in your inbox? Sign up hereGood morning.Donald Trump has condemned an Iranian response to a US peace proposal as “totally unacceptable” as the month-old ceasefire appeared to be wearing thin.What is the US position on Iranian nuclear facilities? The US parameters for nuclear talks reportedly included a moratorium on Iranian nuclear enrichment for up to 20 years; the transfer overseas, possibly to the US, of Iran’s stockpile of highly enriched uranium (HEU), and the dismantling of Iranian nuclear facilities.What is the sticking point? According to the Wall Street Journal, the Iranian counter-proposal suggested a shorter moratorium, the export of part of the highly enriched uranium stockpile and the dilution of the rest, and refusal to accept the dismantling of facilities.How have markets reacted? After Trump rejected the counter-proposal on his Truth Social platform, there was a 4% jump in Brent crude on Monday to $105.50 a barrel, before it settled at $103.50.Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.What measures are being taken to control the spread? As well as one who tested positive, another passenger has mild symptoms of hantavirus, the US health and human services department confirmed. These passengers were travelling in the plane’s biocontainment units, it added.Is the World Health Organization mandating a quarantine? No – it has recommended, but not mandated, a 42-day quarantine once passengers have landed in their home countries. As some countries enforce stricter rules than others, here is how the responses vary. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump rejects Iran's ceasefire proposal response. And, Congress to tackle ICE funding
items.standfirst: Trump rejected Iran's response to a U.S. peace proposal, calling it "totally unacceptable." And, Congressional Republicans are trying to push for three years of funding for immigration enforcement.
items.content: Trump rejected Iran's response to a U.S. peace proposal, calling it "totally unacceptable." And, Congressional Republicans are trying to push for three years of funding for immigration enforcement.(Image credit: Alex Wong)

items.title: Trump rejects Iran's response to US peace proposal as 'unacceptable'
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump on Sunday branded Iran's terms for ending the Middle East war "totally unacceptable," raising the likelihood of renewed conflict after weeks of negotiations. Iran had responded to Washington's latest peace proposal earlier in the day, while warning it would not hold back from retaliating against any new US strikes or permit more foreign warships in the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: US President Donald Trump on Sunday branded Iran's terms for ending the Middle East war "totally unacceptable," raising the likelihood of renewed conflict after weeks of negotiations. Iran had responded to Washington's latest peace proposal earlier in the day, while warning it would not hold back from retaliating against any new US strikes or permit more foreign warships in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Suspected hantavirus case triggers emergency deployment to isolated island
items.standfirst: As Americans are evacuated from the Dutch-flagged cruise ship linked to the hantavirus outbreak, two passengers have now tested positive. But in the South Atlantic, a scene from a Hollywood thriller: paratroopers dropping onto one of the world’s most remote inhabited islands with emergency medical supplies after a suspected case. A British national is now in a stable condition while isolating.
items.content: As Americans are evacuated from the Dutch-flagged cruise ship linked to the hantavirus outbreak, two passengers have now tested positive. But in the South Atlantic, a scene from a Hollywood thriller: paratroopers dropping onto one of the world’s most remote inhabited islands with emergency medical supplies after a suspected case. A British national is now in a stable condition while isolating.

items.title: Hantavirus outbreak: US, French nationals test positive after evacuating ship
items.standfirst: An American citizen and a French woman evacuated from the cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak have tested positive, officials said, as the repatriation operation continued on Monday. Following the positive test results, Spain defended the rigour of its sanitary measures during the complex evacuation Sunday of 94 people of 19 different nationalities from the MV Hondius, which is moored in the Canary Islands. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin reports from Bichat Hospital in central Paris.
items.content: An American citizen and a French woman evacuated from the cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak have tested positive, officials said, as the repatriation operation continued on Monday. Following the positive test results, Spain defended the rigour of its sanitary measures during the complex evacuation Sunday of 94 people of 19 different nationalities from the MV Hondius, which is moored in the Canary Islands. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin reports from Bichat Hospital in central Paris.

items.title: French passenger from hantavirus ship tests positive
items.standfirst: A French woman repatriated from a cruise ship struck by hantavirus has tested positive with the rare disease, France's health minister said Monday, in the country's first case since the outbreak. The woman, one of five French passengers flown back from the MV Hondius and placed in isolation in Paris, started to feel very unwell on Sunday night and "tests came back positive", Health Minister Stephanie Rist told the radio station France Inter. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin reports from Paris, France.
items.content: A French woman repatriated from a cruise ship struck by hantavirus has tested positive with the rare disease, France's health minister said Monday, in the country's first case since the outbreak. The woman, one of five French passengers flown back from the MV Hondius and placed in isolation in Paris, started to feel very unwell on Sunday night and "tests came back positive", Health Minister Stephanie Rist told the radio station France Inter. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin reports from Paris, France.

items.title: Hantavirus: Ship evacuations enter second day
items.standfirst: The last 24 passengers aboard the MV Hondius are set to evacuate the ship on May 11, before the vessel gets disinfected and goes back to the Netherlands. At least two passengers have tested positive since disembarking.
items.content: The last 24 passengers aboard the MV Hondius are set to evacuate the ship on May 11, before the vessel gets disinfected and goes back to the Netherlands. At least two passengers have tested positive since disembarking.

items.title: Crude oil prices rise as Trump calls Iran's offer 'totally unacceptable'
items.standfirst: Benchmark crude oil prices rose on Monday as US President Donald Trump rejected Iran's latest counterproposal to find an end to the war in the Middle East. The continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz is leaving its mark on economies around the world, with India's Prime Minister Narendra Modi calling for reduced fuel use and Chinese inflation gauges ticking up higher than expected. Also in this edition: French President Emmanuel Macron announces more than €1 billion in investment in Kenya.
items.content: Benchmark crude oil prices rose on Monday as US President Donald Trump rejected Iran's latest counterproposal to find an end to the war in the Middle East. The continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz is leaving its mark on economies around the world, with India's Prime Minister Narendra Modi calling for reduced fuel use and Chinese inflation gauges ticking up higher than expected. Also in this edition: French President Emmanuel Macron announces more than €1 billion in investment in Kenya.

clusters.title: Iran Nobel winner Narges Mohammadi released on bail for medical treatment
clusters.neutral_headline: Ailing Iranian Nobel laureate given bail and hospital transfer
bullet_summary: Nobel Peace laureate and activist Narges Mohammadi has been transferred to a Tehran hospital more than a week after collapsing in prison, her foundation said Sunday
Reported by France24, BBC World, NPR News

items.title: Iran Nobel winner Narges Mohammadi released on bail for medical treatment
items.standfirst: Iranian authorities on Sunday released Nobel peace prize winner Narges Mohammadi on bail following growing alarm over her health and she has already been transferred to Tehran for medical treatment, her supporters said. They had last week warned that Mohammadi, who won the 2023 prize in recognition of her decades of campaigning for human rights in Iran, was at risk of dying on prison after suffer two suspected heart attacks behind bars in Zanjan.
items.content: Iranian authorities on Sunday released Nobel peace prize winner Narges Mohammadi on bail following growing alarm over her health and she has already been transferred to Tehran for medical treatment, her supporters said. They had last week warned that Mohammadi, who won the 2023 prize in recognition of her decades of campaigning for human rights in Iran, was at risk of dying on prison after suffer two suspected heart attacks behind bars in Zanjan.

items.standfirst: 1st Lt Kendrick Lamont Key Jr was participating in largest military exercise among US, Nato allies and African nationsA search team recovered the body of a US soldier who went missing near a cliff during a training exercise in Cap Draa, Morocco, the US Army said on Sunday.Moroccan searchers found the remains on Saturday in the water within a mile (1.6km) of where the soldier went missing on 2 May, the army said in a statement. items.title: Trump heads to China this week to meet Xi as Iran war and trade disputes loom over summit – US politics live
items.standfirst: It will be the first time a US president has visited China in nearly a decade, with the last visit being Trump in 2017Sign up for the Breaking News US emailThe US Senate is expected to confirm Kevin Warsh this week as chair of the Federal Reserve, as Donald Trump continues his campaign to influence the world’s most important central bank.The vote is expected to be split along party lines. Democrats criticize Warsh for being Trump’s “sock puppet” at a time when the president has pushed past the typical boundaries between the White House and the nonpartisan Fed. Continue reading...

Democrats criticize Warsh for being Trump’s “sock puppet” at a time when the president has pushed past the typical boundaries between the White House and the nonpartisan Fed. Continue reading...

items.title: US Senate expected to confirm Kevin Warsh as next Federal Reserve chair
items.standfirst: Warsh would succeed outgoing Fed chair Jerome Powell as Trump continues his push to influence the US central bankSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The US Senate is expected to confirm Kevin Warsh this week as chair of the Federal Reserve, as Donald Trump continues his campaign to influence the world’s most important central bank.The Fed’s influence over the economy spans from the job market to mortgage rates, and its every move is carefully scrutinized by investors on Wall Street. Warsh’s confirmation comes at a turbulent time for the central bank, which has fallen under intense scrutiny from Trump for not lowering interest rates. Continue reading...

Warsh’s confirmation comes at a turbulent time for the central bank, which has fallen under intense scrutiny from Trump for not lowering interest rates. Continue reading...
